programmes.show_description: The Trial: In 1966, Brady and Hindley are brought to court. Witnesses describe the trial, and letters reveal the serial killers' prison lives as Britain's longest-serving inmates. (Ep3/3)
for Stand Up To Cancer: Joe Wilkinson, Roisin Conaty, Rose Matafeo, Judi Love, Tom Davis and Jon Richardson recreate the weirdest places that they've ever slept, in cake form!
Love can strike at any age. Or so we hope. The daters this time include single mum Maisie and guarded Dan, Cornwall romantics Mark and Josie, and gaming duo Joe and Rebecca.
Pansexual sex therapist Mariia's after a not-so-average Mr or Mrs who's not too tired for sex. Old romantic at heart Alannah seeks a forever fella who can speak her love language.
Some extra brunch as Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer host the food and chat show.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Phil Spencer goes house-hunting in south Manchester's desirable suburbs with Craig and Becki, while Kirstie Allsopp searches the rolling Cheshire countryside with Chris and Amy.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programmes.show_description: Matt Baker and his family prepare for a busy season at the farm. They get hands-on for lambing season and there's exciting news for the goats.
Bedfordshire and Cambridgeshire. Michaela hosts the final night. Shane thinks he's got it in the bag - but will Michaela's Italian menu make him think again? (Ep5/5)
It's payment day: a chance for the B&Bers to complain about specks of dust that they've somehow found after trawling their rooms with a high-powered magnifying glass.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In Italy, Angie and Ash rush to finish off their renovations. In Spain, business booms for Daniel and John, but will rookie bakers Rachael and Lee rise to the challenge?
Nikki and Gordon want a home with great views on the Maltese island of Gozo. Ben Hillman shows them what their £350k can buy. Has he found the Mediterranean dream?

programmes.show_name: A Place in the Sun: What...
programmes.show_description: ...Happened Next?: Jasmine Harman sees how Gary and Lynn are getting on in Haute-Vienne. And are Lorna and Brian's Almería villa and its pool still making a splash?
Winchester. Live wire Ptol is convinced that high-quality food and fine wine will win him the prize. But his guests find his menu underwhelming and his hosting style over the top. (Ep1/5)
Winchester. DJ Francis Leigh serves a Thai meal with a twist, on a weird and wonderful night featuring dead flowers on the table and a song about spring rolls. (Ep2/5)
Winchester. Patricia plans a Latin party with Peruvian food, on a night of singing and dancing. But Ptol mistakenly thinks he's eating llama instead of lamb... (Ep3/5)
Winchester. Care home worker Georgia Groves hosts a black-tie affair, complete with red carpet, posh frocks... and a spray tan booth. (Ep4/5)
Winchester. Estate agent William Tilsley serves up a vegetarian menu and a blind-tasting food game. But one guest ends up spitting wine over another... (Ep5/5)
Vicky and Carl have brought their retirement plans forward and are now ready to move to Spain. Jean Johansson shows them what their £70k can buy on the southern Costa Blanca.
Kazal and Eleena are ditching Dubai and moving back home to Solihull to be close to family. Can they overcome building blunders to create the ultimate hosting hub?
We meet Brixham's maverick skipper who live-streams his latest catch, and a Cornwall engineer gets a replica of the iconic steam locomotive, The Puffing Devil, back up to full steam.

A pedestrian is rushed to A&E with multiple life-threatening injuries after a hit-and-run. An acrobat has a damaged wrist. And a 93-year-old has a broken leg.
Heather, who's 65, is rushed to Queen's after collapsing in the park. Long-serving member of the team Deanna speaks of her previous struggles with addiction.
The Goggleboxers settle in for more of the biggest and best TV. What do they think this time?
This time the Goggleboxers watch Married at First Sight Australia, The Summit, LOL: Last One Laughing UK, Boarders, I Swear, Wheel of Fortune and Melania.
(2023) Brandon Cronenberg's intense psychological horror with Alexander Skarsgård and Mia Goth. When a rich tourist accidentally kills someone, he gets cloned to avoid the death penalty.
...Experiment: Tom and Kirsty check in to fulfil Tom's fantasy of experimenting with another man. Natalie and Tom explore a threesome, but will insecurities get in the way?

Merseyside. Fun-loving oil and gas co-ordinator Danielle Greenwood hopes to impress her guests with her down-to-earth menu and warm and welcoming style of hosting. (Ep1/5)
Merseyside. Pastoral care manager Steven Booth names each of his courses after songs by his favourite singer, Tina Turner. But tensions rise when one guest puts her foot in it. (Ep2/5)
Merseyside. Glamorous PR professional Jade Chantelle places her bets on a casino-themed evening featuring a high-class menu, lashings of champagne and a game of roulette. (Ep3/5)
Merseyside. Primary school teacher Anna Senior plans a fairy-tale evening, featuring a Mad Hatter's soup and a special pork dish cooked three ways. (Ep4/5)
Merseyside. Michael Bublé impersonator Jamie Somers hosts the final night. Can Jamie and Jade overcome their differences? (Ep5/5)
In the West Country, Kirstein hosts a Greek evening of hearty home cooking with a dash of tea towel tossing and terrific togas. Will her Grecian get-together appease her guests? (Ep1/5)
In the West Country, Peppe hosts an authentic Italian night, but one guest doesn't like Italian food! Will his Neapolitan nosh impress, or will hamster high jinks hamper things? (Ep2/5)
In the West Country, barber Natalia hopes that her 90s night will be a cut above the rest. But with controversial comments and nitpicking, will it be more disaster than nostalgia? (Ep3/5)
In the West Country, Richard puts on some top-notch British bistro fare, but his tagliatelle leaves one guest tongue-tied. Will it be a sophisticated soiree or dull as dishwater? (Ep4/5)
The new run continues. In the West Country, spiritualist Jo plans a divine dinner of international fare. But will pizza profanities and ghostly goings-on ruin her chances? (Ep5/5)

Kirstie and Phil visit west London, where property is expensive and sells fast. They help newly-weds Keon and Zoe in Shepherd's Bush, and Paul and Carolyn in Richmond.

programmes.show_description: ...Happened Next?: On Malta, we see Chris' new kitchen from Italy no less! On the Costa Blanca, we revisit Jan and Bill at Casa Milly, that they named in honour of Bill's mum.
